Incident Reference: ML-IR-2026-0517 — MettaLok Industries Limited (listed on the National Stock Exchange of Bharat, NSEB).
On 16 May 2026, a cyber incident in the consent manager of our HR/payroll system “SwiftLedger Pro” led to unauthorised access to DPDPA personal data.
The exposure included identifiers (Aadhaar and PAN fields), minor-dependent details, and certain UPSI relating to draft Q1 results and a confidential divestment term sheet.
Ms. Nisha Kapoor (Employee ID ML-44812) had her fingerprint template from the biometric attendance module and a clinic note about treatment for endometriosis accessed without consent.
Master Rohan Dev, age 16 (dependent of Mr. Shashank Dev), had his vaccination history and school insurance enrolment references viewed in the same cache.
Mr. Aarav Balan’s file incorporated a genetic screening report for a thalassemia carrier marker together with his union membership record in the MettaLok Workers Forum.
An external processor, LotusPay Prime Pvt. Ltd., briefly received a spreadsheet linking employee records to UPSI on projected EBITDA and a not-yet-announced plant sale, breaching our PIT Code 2024.
Although we are an equal opportunity employer with DEI commitments, the root cause was a misconfigured SSO scope for a service account in Indapur and not any policy choice.
No Aadhaar, PAN, or GSTIN numbers were fully visible; logs show placeholders like Aadhaar XXXX-1313-7788 and PAN XXPXX1234Q rendered only to audit viewers.
A benign clarification: the word union here also refers to the union of two datasets in a Spark job, not any person’s trade-union detail.
UPSI handling follows our Internal UPSI Handling Standard and PIT Code drafted per Indian securities regulator guidance, but an email rule forwarded previews to a Kavapur staging mailbox.
As a metaphor, the breach spread like a cracked monsoon umbrella across shared drives while a test bot named BluePanda mirrored folders.
We note a separate benign reference to sexual orientation in a generic staff survey template, not tied to named individuals, resided adjacent to affected folders.
Pursuant to our Data Fiduciary Consent Governance Policy 2024 and the DPDPA, notices are being issued to impacted Data Principals and to the NSEB Monitoring Cell.
Contact for queries: compliance@mettalok.in and Mr. R. Raghavan (DPO), +91-00000-00000, who will coordinate with the securities compliance officer for PIT follow-up.
For avoidance of doubt, there is no connection to local sports fan groups like the Kavapur Kites or the Avanya Tigers referenced in sample data.
Status: contained; access tokens revoked, keys rotated, and a preliminary intimation filed with the Bharat Securities Authority under Section 3 of our Incident Reporting SOP.
